title: gaultheria microphylla
intro: Gaultheria microphylla, also known as Mountain Pepper or Pōhuehue, is a type of flowering plant native to New Zealand and eastern Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Gaultheria microphylla include its ability to aid in digestion, relieve pain and inflammation, and support the immune system. Gaultheria microphylla can be prepared as a herbal tea, infused oil, or tincture, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
